# KDE Plasma + Wayland — friction points to watch for with Handy

**Captured:** 14/04/26
**Generated by:** Claude (Opus 4.6), in conversation with Daniel — plausible-failure-modes brainstorm, not verified against the running app.
**Status:** Watch-list. Promote any item to a proper entry under `bugs/` when reproduced, or to its own `ideas/` entry if it's a feature request.

## Purpose

A catalogue of the ways desktop dictation tooling tends to go wrong on KDE Plasma + Wayland, specifically as they might affect [Handy](https://github.com/cjpais/Handy). Each item is a hypothesis to verify against real behaviour — do not file upstream without reproducing.

## The watch-list

### 1. Global hotkey registration

On Wayland, apps can't do raw X11 key grabs. The supported paths are:

- **XDG Desktop Portal Global Shortcuts** (`org.freedesktop.portal.GlobalShortcuts`) — the cross-desktop way.
- **KDE's KGlobalAccel** — the native KDE way.

**Symptom to watch for:** PTT or toggle hotkey works only when Handy's own window is focused; triggers twice; fires when you don't want it; or steals keystrokes from other apps.

### 2. Active-window detection before injection

Wayland denies external processes the ability to query the currently focused window (security model — no X11 `_NET_ACTIVE_WINDOW` equivalent). Tools that rely on focus-awareness have to either (a) trust that focus hasn't moved, or (b) use compositor-specific protocols.

**Symptom to watch for:** Transcript injected into the wrong window after tab-switching mid-dictation, or into the window that was focused when PTT started rather than the one focused when the transcript arrives. See [`focus-loss-during-dictation`](https://github.com/danielrosehill/Live-Typing-UX-Research/blob/main/ideas/focus-loss-during-dictation.md) in the research workspace for the general pattern.

### 3. `wtype` flakiness on kwin specifically

`wtype` works well on wlroots compositors (Sway, Hyprland). On kwin_wayland its support is partial — modifier-key handling is patchy and some kwin versions silently drop events.

**Symptom to watch for:** Typing Tool `Auto` picking `wtype` on KDE and producing intermittent failures. Forcing `ydotool` is the usual fix.

### 4. `xdotool` silently degrades under Wayland

`xdotool` only interacts with Xwayland clients. Native Wayland apps (most of KDE Plasma 6, modern GTK4/Qt6 apps) are invisible to it.

**Symptom to watch for:** Injection works in Firefox-on-Xwayland but not in KDE-native apps. If Handy's `Auto` ever selects `xdotool`, it's the wrong default on KDE Wayland — expect silent failure.

### 5. Text-input-v3 coverage gaps

`zwp_text_input_v3` is the Wayland protocol for IME-style commit-string input. kwin implements it; client support varies:

- **Good:** GTK4, Qt6.
- **Patchy:** Electron (historically poor; improving in recent versions).
- **Varies:** terminal emulators — Konsole ok, some foot/alacritty setups don't.

**Symptom to watch for:** If Handy ever ships an IME backend, per-app behaviour will diverge.

### 6. Fcitx5 interference

Daniel runs Fcitx5 for Hebrew. Fcitx5 sits between synthesised keystrokes and the target app — it can swallow or re-compose characters, especially dead keys, combining diacritics, and modifier sequences.

**Symptom to watch for:** Dead-key or composition glitches in apps like Konsole or VS Code when dictating with Fcitx5 active. May disappear if Fcitx5 is disabled or set to a passthrough mode.

### 7. Clipboard "owner dies" race

`wl-copy` holds the Wayland clipboard by staying alive as a background process until someone else takes ownership. If a paste pipeline spawns `wl-copy` and exits before the target app reads the clipboard, the clipboard can appear empty when Ctrl+V fires.

**Symptom to watch for:** Intermittent empty pastes. Mitigation: `wl-copy --foreground`, or integrate with a clipboard manager (klipper) so the content persists.

### 8. Layer-shell overlays / HUD rendering

Status indicators or HUD overlays on KDE Wayland must use `wlr-layer-shell` (supported by kwin) or `org.kde.plasma.surface`. Legacy X11 override-redirect tricks don't survive the port.

**Symptom to watch for:** Handy's dictation-status indicator rendering behind windows, not appearing at all, or appearing in the wrong location.

### 9. SDDM + Plasma Wayland autostart ordering

Apps autostarted with systray icons on KDE Plasma under SDDM sometimes fail silently on first login because the Wayland socket or StatusNotifierHost isn't ready when the app starts.

**Symptom to watch for:** Handy autostarts but has no tray icon / global hotkey until manually restarted.

### 10. PipeWire mic-source live switching

Not strictly Wayland, but bundled with modern Plasma — PipeWire handles audio routing. Apps that cache the initial mic source don't always respond to `pavucontrol` / KDE audio-applet mid-session changes.

**Symptom to watch for:** Swapping input devices in the audio applet doesn't change which mic Handy records from until restart.

## How to use this list

- Reproduce before filing. Handy may already handle any given item correctly.
- If reproduced and it's a broken promise → promote to `bugs/<slug>.md` and upstream to [cjpais/Handy](https://github.com/cjpais/Handy/issues).
- If it's a missing capability → promote to its own `ideas/<slug>.md` with motivation and implementation notes.

## Provenance

This list was brainstormed by an AI agent (Claude Opus 4.6) based on general familiarity with Wayland and KDE ecosystem pitfalls. It is **not** a verified defect list for Handy — items may already be handled, may be misattributed, or may be non-issues on current versions. Treat as a testing checklist, not a bug manifest.
